---
id: app-webhook-create
title: "Register an incoming webhook for a specific application"
description: "[Register an incoming webhook](https://devcenter.bitrise.io/api/incoming-and-outgoing-webhooks/#incoming-webhooks) for a specific application. You can do this during the app registration process or at any other time in an app's life. When calling this endpoint, a webhook is registered at your git provider: this is necessary to automatically trigger builds on Bitrise."
sidebar_label: "Register an incoming webhook for a specific application"
hide_table_of_contents: true
api: 
sidebar_class_name: "post api-method"
info_path: en/bitrise-api/api-reference/bitrise-api
custom_edit_url: null
---

import MethodEndpoint from "@theme/ApiExplorer/MethodEndpoint";
import ParamsDetails from "@theme/ParamsDetails";
import RequestSchema from "@theme/RequestSchema";
import StatusCodes from "@theme/StatusCodes";
import OperationTabs from "@theme/OperationTabs";
import TabItem from "@theme/TabItem";
import Heading from "@theme/Heading";
import Translate from "@docusaurus/Translate";

<Heading
  as={"h1"}
  className={"openapi__heading"}
  children={"Register an incoming webhook for a specific application"}
>
</Heading>

<MethodEndpoint
  method={"post"}
  path={"/apps/{app-slug}/register-webhook"}
  context={"endpoint"}
>
  
</MethodEndpoint>



[Register an incoming webhook](https://devcenter.bitrise.io/api/incoming-and-outgoing-webhooks/#incoming-webhooks) for a specific application. You can do this during the app registration process or at any other time in an app's life. When calling this endpoint, a webhook is registered at your git provider: this is necessary to automatically trigger builds on Bitrise.

<Heading
  id={"request"}
  as={"h2"}
  className={"openapi-tabs__heading"}
>
  <Translate id="theme.openapi.request.title">Request</Translate>
</Heading>
